$note: Brief 155 — B11 Stage 3 web-enrichment PROPOSALS. READ-ONLY. NO APPLY PATH: no script, db:rebuild, seed loader, or DB write reads this file. Materialization is the maintainer/Codex hand-gates ONLY — Gate F (factions → factions.json + faction-aliases.json) and Gate L (locations → locations.json + location-aliases.json). Fields are evidenced from cited web sources or left null — never guessed. The reviewer writes proposals, never truth.

tones: lore-ambiguous Fallen Angels, pragmatically under Chaos

factions.notes: Loyalist, Codex-compliant Blood Angels successor of the 9th Founding; artisan/enlightenment chapter culture, free of the Black Rage. The Reverie depicts them as the Angels Resplendent (pre-transformation); they later became the puritanical Angels Penitent, which is the title the Lexicanum article now redirects to. Parent set to blood_angels (cited Blood Angels lineage; catalog precedent sanguinary_guard->blood_angels).

Renegade Chapter founded mid-M35; under Chapter Master Lugft Huron they seceded and triggered the Badab War, drawing in the Mantis Warriors (the book's frame). NOT aliased to red_corsairs: Lexicanum keeps separate articles for the Astral Claws (the original Chapter) and the Red Corsairs (the later piratical warband formed from the survivors), so they are distinct canonical entities. Modelled as seceded Astartes (parent heretic_astartes, alignment chaos) consistent with the catalog's red_corsairs entry.

Loyalist, Codex-compliant Imperial Fists successor (unknown Founding). The World Engine recounts their sacrificial assault under Chapter Master Artor Amhrad, who rammed the battle-barge Tempestus into the Necron World Engine; the entire 772-strong Chapter was annihilated. Parent imperial_fists per cited Lexicanum lineage (catalog precedent crimson_fists->imperial_fists).

Loyalist Space Marine Chapter of the 'Cursed' 21st Founding with unstable gene-seed (bone-blade mutations / 'Dragon Claws'); censured by the Inquisition but remains loyal, matching The Death of Antagonis. Parent left as the generic adeptus_astartes: the Salamanders successor link is described only as 'long rumoured', so a specific salamanders parent is not evidenced and is deliberately not claimed.

One of the major Ork Klanz (the first clan to encounter the Imperium; known for Kommandos and looted vehicles). Canonical Ork sub-faction, parent 'orks', alignment xenos / tone alien matching catalog siblings goffs and freebooterz. Matches the 'Da Genrul of the Blood Axes' contender in Warboss (W40K-0128). Not in factions.json or faction-aliases.json.

Codex-compliant Imperial Space Marine Chapter; provides the boarding force (with the Star Dragons) under Inquisitor Remigius in Accursed Eternity (W40K-0308). Loyalist Astartes, parent 'adeptus_astartes', alignment imperium / tone imperial. Not in factions.json or faction-aliases.json.

Codex Chapter hailing from the jungle world of Tauron, renowned for blunt head-on warfare; Captain Daed's Chapter that fights alongside the Raven Guard in The Unkindness of Ravens (W40K-0332) and Sons of Corax (W40K-0428). Distinct entity from the separate 'Minotaurs' Chapter (own dedicated Lexicanum page). Loyalist Astartes, parent 'adeptus_astartes', alignment imperium / tone imperial. Not in factions.json or faction-aliases.json.

Space Marine Chapter; though a renegade splinter exists in their lore, the loyalist Crimson Castellans are the protagonist force holding Ector in the Valedan/Valedor system against Hive Fleet Leviathan in The Last Days of Ector (W40K-0341). Catalogued as loyalist Astartes, parent 'adeptus_astartes', alignment imperium / tone imperial. Not in factions.json or faction-aliases.json.

The Crimson Sabres are the loyalist predecessor of the Crimson Slaughter — the same Chapter before its fall in the Eye of Terror (the book 'Crimson Dawn' is explicitly 'the origin of one of the galaxy's most feared renegade warbands'). Lexicanum treats 'Crimson Sabres' as a redirect to its 'Crimson Slaughter' page, i.e. one canonical entity. crimson_slaughter already exists in the catalog, so dedup → alias rather than a duplicate.

One of the major Ork klanz, defined by its devotion to speed ('Da red wunz go fasta'). Directly parallel to the existing goffs entry (parent orks, xenos/alien). Not in factions.json or faction-aliases.json. Zagnob Thundaskuzz in 'Warboss' is an Evil Sunz contender.

Codex-compliant loyalist Chapter, a Second Founding successor of the Imperial Fists (homeworld Eschara, Segmentum Obscurus). Parent set to imperial_fists, matching the catalog's crimson_fists precedent for a direct Imperial Fists successor. Protagonist Chapter of 'Legion of the Damned' (Kersh's force). Not in factions.json or faction-aliases.json.

'Fire Masters' is the byname of Legio Suturvora, a traitor Titan Legion of the Collegia Titanica/Adeptus Titanicus that turned at the Battle of Calth (the on-page antagonist engines in 'Honour to the Dead', against the loyalist Legio Praesagius). canonicalName uses the formal Legio name to match the catalog's 'Legio X' titan-legion pattern (legio_mortis, legio_vulpa, legio_audax); glyph carries the book's 'Fire Masters' surface form. parent adeptus_titanicus, chaos/engine (cf. legio_vulpa, legio_audax). Not in factions.json or faction-aliases.json.

Canonical loyalist Ultramarines Successor Chapter raised in the Ultima Founding, composed entirely of Primaris Marines; blue power armour, which matches the 'blue-armoured strangers' / Fulminators Reivers under Sergeant Augustin in 'Lords of the Storm'. Parent set to adeptus_astartes per catalog convention for Astartes Chapters; alignment imperium, tone imperial as a loyalist Codex Chapter.

Canonical human stellar empire of eleven inhabited worlds (near the Dominion of Storms, Ultima Segmentum / Eastern Fringe), grown strong through the Age of Strife and ruled by a warlike aristocracy. During 'Ferrus Manus: The Gorgon of Medusa' it is the bitterly NON-compliant opposing power Ferrus Manus brings to compliance (the Fall of the Lords of Gardinaal). It is a purely human, independent (non-Imperial, non-Chaos, non-xenos) civilisation at the time of the novel, so alignment leaves no clean fit in the vocab and stays null rather than being guessed; it is a top-level power, so parent is null.

Canonical loyalist Ultramarines Successor Chapter, the first Ultramarines successor, raised in the Second Founding from veterans who survived the Horus Heresy; red power armour, dogmatically loyal to Guilliman and often fighting alongside the Ultramarines. Matches the Genesis Chapter defending the relic on Quradim in 'Of Honour and Iron'. Parent adeptus_astartes, alignment imperium, tone imperial.

Canonical loyalist Space Marine Chapter introduced in the novel 'Black Leviathan'; a secretive, ritualistic, clannish Chapter whose hunt for the Black Leviathan (Infinity Worm) overrides all other priorities, and who hold little respect for the Imperium they nonetheless serve. Matches the Jade Dragons manipulated by the T'au Water Caste operative in 'Black Leviathan'. Their gene-seed lineage / Founding is undocumented/secretive in the sources, so parent is set to the generic adeptus_astartes rather than a guessed Legion; alignment imperium (loyalist), tone imperial.

Loyalist Space Marine Chapter (Marauders successor) that sided with the rebel Astral Claws/Maelstrom Warders in the Badab War, was granted the Emperor's forgiveness and sent on a penitent crusade — matches the source book 'The Trial of the Mantis Warrior' exactly. Alignment imperium (pardoned loyalists, not Chaos). Lexicanum/Fandom say they are 'believed' descended from the White Scars via the Marauders, but that lineage is hedged/speculative, so parent stays the generic adeptus_astartes rather than a guessed white_scars.

Loyalist Codex Chapter, an Ultramarines Second Founding successor and one of the Shield Chapters of Ultramar; gene-seed wholly consistent with the Ultramarines. First Chapter Master Lucretius Corvo fought the Word Bearers at Astagar. Parent ultramarines (the progenitor Chapter exists in the catalog). Matches 'Death of Integrity' where the Novamarines co-star with the Blood Drinkers.

Loyalist Codex Chapter (homeworld Draconith) that supplies half the boarding party in the source book 'Accursed Eternity' alongside the Blood Swords — Lexicanum confirms the Chapter and the novella role. Lexicanum explicitly frames descent from Ferrus Manus/Iron Hands as 'speculation' (and a forum claim of Salamanders gene-seed is non-canonical fan fluff), so the progenitor is unevidenced and parent stays the generic adeptus_astartes rather than a guessed Legion.

Loyalist Ultramarines successor Chapter (Chapter 281, founded 546.M32 in the wake of the War of the Beast, born from Ultramarines gene-stock) that watches over Segmentum Pacificus — matches the four-part Tome Keepers campaign in the 'Blood of the Imperium' anthology. Parent ultramarines (progenitor exists in the catalog). Sector is a faction-level field that does not apply here.

Real Officio Assassinorum temple (the intelligence/info-cyte arm; originally the Clade Vanus). Matches the source book: the third core operative Avaaris Koln is a Vanus analyst. Catalog already holds the sibling temples (vindicare_temple, callidus_temple, culexus_temple, eversor_temple) under officio_assassinorum but lacks Vanus -> new. Parent officio_assassinorum, alignment imperium, tone shadow to match the other temples.

locations.notes: Imperial hive world, principal setting of The Ember Wolves (HH); loyalist Adeptus Mechanicus / Legio Castigatra vs traitor Dark Mechanicum / Legio Audax engine war, incl. Princeps Balthus Voltemand and the Warmonger Tantorus Magnificat -- matches the synopsis exactly. Lexicanum titles the article 'Absolom' (book/prose spelling 'Absalom'); recommend an alias 'Absalom' -> absolom. Located in the 'Gorgonopsii Maestrale region of space', which is not one of the vocab segmenta -> sector null. No documented galactic position -> placeable false, coords null.

Imperial Civilised World, rotationally (tidally) locked -- sun-baked desert hemisphere, arctic night hemisphere, population in the equatorial 'Shadow Belt' twilight strip; principal setting of The Traitor's Hand (Ciaphas Cain), defended against the Chaos Ravagers raiding fleet and a Slaaneshi cult. Matches the synopsis. Lexicanum does not state a segmentum; the Ravagers staged from an unidentified Ultima Segmentum subsector but the source flags this as not definitive for Adumbria's own location -> sector left null (not guessed). No documented galactic position -> placeable false, coords null.

Eldar Maiden World that is also a Necron Tomb World, of spiritual significance to Craftworld Saim-Hann; at the end of M41 the Saim-Hann/Ynnari race to reclaim it from the reawakened Necrons (Overlord Hazepkhut) -- the principal battlefield of Wild Rider, matching the synopsis. Segmentum not documented in the sources -> sector null (not guessed). No documented galactic position -> placeable false, coords null. Tags eldar + necrons reflect the dual maiden-world/tomb-world status.

Jungle world, the principal setting of 'Hell's Last' (Cadian 101st / Minka Lesk), located in the Gallows Cluster. Lexicanum (Potence) places the Gallows Cluster on the edges of the Segmentum Solar, so sector=solar via the cluster. No documented galactic coordinates -> placeable=false. Recent (2025) novel world; sector is cluster-derived, hence moderate confidence.

Half-shattered colony/city, the on-page setting of 'Perpetual', where Oll Persson's band is stranded and the Alpha Legion sets its trap. Lexicanum (Ollanius Persson) records Oll reaching the city of Andrioch at the tail end of M23 during the rebellion of the Iron Men. No Segmentum/sector or galactic position documented -> sector=null, placeable=false. Existence well-evidenced.

Dark volcanic world, central setting of 'I Am Slaughter' (Beast Arises). Lexicanum places it in the Segmentum Solar, ~6 weeks warp travel from Terra. The Imperial Fists were decimated there fighting the Chromes; the Ork Attack Moon then shattered/consumed the planet -> destroyed=true. No documented galactic coordinates -> placeable=false. Tags reflect Imperial Fists + Orks (the Beast).

Desolate, daemon-haunted artifact world, the dark culmination of 'Empire of Lies' (Farsight trilogy). Lexicanum: pre-Imperial faceless statues, later an Alpha Legion / Chaos-cult world, purged by the Scythes of the Emperor in M39; Farsight fought Khornate daemons there. Located on the Eastern Fringe / in T'au space -> Segmentum Ultima (sector=ultima). Chaos/daemon nature -> tags=['chaos']. No documented coordinates -> placeable=false.

Lexicanum: Subsector Aurelia is a cluster of worlds in the Korianis sector of the Segmentum Ultima, the Blood Ravens' recruiting region and the setting of the entire Dawn of War II trilogy (Calderis, Typhon Primaris, Meridian). The synopsis frames the book across 'the embattled worlds of the Aurelia sub-sector', so the canonical entity is the subsector (a region), not the long-lost capital world Aurelia which vanished into the Warp ~1000 years before DoW II. Sector mapped to the Segmentum Ultima (ultima); no galactic coordinates documented, so placeable=false, coords null.

Lexicanum has a dedicated Bacchus page: an Imperial agri world of swampland famed for wine, located in the Yossarian Subsector (capital Hedon), the sole setting of Outgunned where Imperial forces fight an escalating feral-ork incursion. Yossarian Subsector is not in the controlled sector vocab and no parent Segmentum is documented, so sector stays null (honest). Tags imperium + orks reflect the documented Imperial allegiance and the central ork war. No galactic position documented; placeable=false, coords null.

Lexicanum's The Child Foretold short-story page confirms Ballard's Run as an Imperial agri world blighted by an invasive 'warrior weed' and worked by farmers with a planetary militia — the sole principal setting of the novella. No dedicated planet article and no documented Segmentum/sector, so sector stays null. The ork incursion in the synopsis is the protagonist's backstory (lost his family a decade earlier), not on-world, so no orks tag; only imperium. No galactic position documented; placeable=false, coords null.

Lexicanum Bastion (Planet): a heavily militarised human world that held neutrality in the early Horus Heresy, the setting of Forgotten Sons (Nick Kyme). At the story's end an Iron Warriors saboteur detonates the Cullis thermo-nuclear hub, triggering a chain reaction that destroys the planet — hence destroyed=true. No Segmentum/sector documented, so sector stays null. The 'capital' in the synopsis is Bastion's capital city, not Bastion being a capital world, so capital=null. Tags empty: the world's defining trait is neutrality, owned by no faction. No coordinates documented; placeable=false.

Imperial mining world, principal setting of the novel Iron Guard (Mark Clapham); the 114th Mordian Iron Guard are deployed there against an alien-origin affliction spreading through the Belmosian population. Existence confirmed via the Lexicanum novel page (no dedicated world page surfaced, but the world is the unambiguous canonical setting). No segmentum/sector documented -> sector null; no galactic position documented -> placeable false, coords null. Tagged imperium (Imperial colony) + astra_militarum (Mordian Iron Guard garrison defines its in-story role).

Feudal World (designated 'Twenty-Eight One'), the world of Fulgrim's first solo Imperial Compliance, principal setting of Fulgrim: The Palatine Phoenix (Josh Reynolds). Dedicated Lexicanum page exists. Great Crusade era Imperial compliance world brought in by the Emperor's Children -> tags imperium + emperors_children. No segmentum/sector documented -> sector null; no galactic position -> placeable false, coords null.

World (originally 'Ibsen', Great Crusade designation 154-4) brought into Compliance by Vulkan's Salamanders; its xenos-corrupted population was purged by flame and the scoured Dead World renamed Caldera, a world Vulkan swore to protect. Climactic setting of The Hunt for Vulkan (Beast Arises #7) where Vulkan returns and an ork invasion (attack-moon conversion) is repelled. Dedicated Lexicanum page exists. Tags imperium + salamanders (Vulkan's protected world) + orks (Beast-era invader). Survived the invasion -> destroyed null (it is a 'Dead World' classification, not annihilated). No segmentum/sector documented -> sector null; no galactic position -> placeable false, coords null.

Former Imperial world (with its moon Thapes Quo) claimed by the T'au Empire's Fifth Sphere of Expansion; the human population was turned by the Water Caste and a later insurrection rose against the T'au. Principal setting of Elemental Council (Noah Van Nguyen). Dedicated Lexicanum page exists. Tags tau (current T'au control) + imperium (former Imperial world / human insurrection). Sector 'ultima' is INFERRED, not stated on the Cao Quo page: the Fifth Sphere of Expansion is documented as operating in Segmentum Ultima and T'au territory is canonically Ultima/Eastern Fringe; verifier should blank if it judges this thin. No galactic position documented -> placeable false, coords null.

Iron Warriors fortress/forge world ruled by Warsmith Andraaz; principal setting of 'The Siege of Castellax' (Waaagh! Biglug, 905.M41). Documented as near the Eye of Terror and supplying the Iron Warriors' lines to Medrengard, but no source cleanly assigns a Segmentum/sector, so sector stays null (sector-only would over-narrow). No documented galactic position -> placeable false, coords null.

Imperial Agri-World, principal setting of 'The Oubliette' (the world Ashielle Matkosen inherits and rules). Lexicanum places it on the galactic south-east edges of Segmentum Tempestus (in Sector Ephialtis, which is not in the controlled sector vocab) -> sector 'tempestus'. Feeds the Adeptus Mechanicus (nutrient paste) -> mechanicus tag. No documented coordinates -> placeable false.

Adeptus Ministorum Cemetery World (dedicated to Ecclesiarch Umberto II), principal setting of 'Legion of the Damned' where the Excoriators defend it against the Cholercaust Blood Crusade. Documented as lying in the Praga Sub-sector of Segmentum Obscurus (sub-sector not in vocab) -> sector 'obscurus'. Capital is Obsequa City but the world itself is not a capital world -> capital null. No documented coordinates -> placeable false.

Star system named in 'Spear of Ultramar' as the site where Warsmith Khrossus's lone Iron Warriors company springs a delaying trap on Guilliman's Ultramarines fleet during the Heresy march toward Terra. Existence is canonical (named in the novella, documented on its Lexicanum page) but only the engagement is detailed -> no Segmentum/sector documented (sector null) and no galactic position (placeable false). Tagged iron_warriors as the force that held/trapped the system.

Imperial ice world, central setting of 'Ice Guard' (Valhallan Ice Warriors). Lexicanum: once-productive world turned to an ice world by a Chaos-fuelled rebellion; population evacuated and the planet subjected to exterminatus (virus-bombed) — destroyed: true is well-evidenced. Segmentum/sector is 'Segmentum Unknown' on Lexicanum, so sector stays null; no documented galactic position, placeable: false, coords null.

Imperial mining / promethium world, the principal setting of 'Fulgrim: The Perfect Son'. Lexicanum 'Crucible (World)': invaded early in M42 by the Emperor's Children under Fulgrim, defended by Cadians, Kasrkin, PDF and Black Templars; the world fell and converted to Chaos but is not recorded as destroyed (destroyed: null). Identity is Imperial at the time of the book → tags ['imperium']. No documented Segmentum/sector → sector null; no documented position → placeable: false, coords null.

Imperial cemetery world (designated Mausoleum Valorum by the Ecclesiarchy), central setting of 'Deus Encarmine'; Blood Angels defended it against a Word Bearers invasion led by Iskavan the Hated. Lexicanum places it in the Ultima Segmentum → sector 'ultima' (sector-level confidence moderate, existence high). Word Bearers were driven off; not destroyed → destroyed: null. No documented galactic position → placeable: false, coords null. tags reflect the Imperial cemetery world defended by the Blood Angels.

Imperial human colony / frontier agri-world, a setting the Eisenhorn 'Xenos' trail crosses (parallel to the already-cataloged Thracian Primaris). Lexicanum/Fandom: colonised in 700.M40 and lies on the outer edge of the Helican Sub-sector → sector 'helican' (consistent with the existing scarus/helican Eisenhorn cluster). No documented galactic position → placeable: false, coords null. Not destroyed → destroyed: null. tags ['imperium'].

Imperial desert world, principal setting of Kasrkin (Edoardo Albert). Defended by Cadian Kasrkin (Astra Militarum), besieged by the T'au Empire. Lexicanum lists it under 'Category:Segmentum Unknown' — no documented segmentum/sector, so sector stays null. No documented galactic position → placeable false, coords null. Not present in locations.json or location-aliases.json.

Imperial world torn by civil war (rebelling civilians vs. Imperial soldiers), principal setting of Above and Beyond (Denny Flowers), sequel to Outgunned. Aerial-combat war with Imperial fighter aces. No documented segmentum/sector → sector null; no documented coordinates → placeable false, coords null. Not present in locations.json or location-aliases.json.

Isolated Imperial forge world (Adeptus Mechanicus), principal setting of Carcharodons: Void Exile (Robbie MacNiven, 2025); the Carcharodons make a stand alongside the Mechanicus against a Chaos-warped space hulk under datagnost Voldire/Vashtorr. DISTINCT from the existing catalog entry 'diamat' (Diamat, a separate Horus-Heresy-era forge world in the Tanagra System) — not an alias. No documented segmentum/sector → sector null; no documented coordinates → placeable false, coords null. Not present in locations.json or location-aliases.json.

Imperial shrine world, principal setting of Mephiston: Blood of Sanguinius (Darius Hinks); cut off from the Astronomican and gripped by religious civil war over the Blade Petrific; Mephiston and a Blood Angels honour guard journey there. DISTINCT from the existing catalog entry 'dominicus_prime' — not an alias. The 'veil' cutting it from the Astronomican is a plot anomaly, not evidence the world itself is a warp region → warp left null. No documented segmentum/sector → sector null; no documented coordinates → placeable false, coords null. Not present in locations.json or location-aliases.json.

Necron Crown World in the Ghoul Stars, the principal setting of the novella 'Severed' (Nate Crowley) — a dead crownworld whose dynasty never properly woke. Existence solid (dedicated Lexicanum page). Sector set to 'ultima' on sector-level plausibility: the Ghoul Stars are documented as lying in the northern part of / northeast of Segmentum Ultima, beyond the Eastern Fringe; this is the nearest Segmentum, though the region is framed as beyond known space (moderate confidence on sector). No galactic coordinates documented -> placeable=false, gx/gy null.

Imperial Knight World, the sole setting of 'Assassinorum: Kingmaker' (Robert Rath): a secessionist Knight World whose king the Officio Assassinorum is sent to remove. Existence confirmed via the Lexicanum novel page and Black Library copy. No Segmentum/sector documented -> sector null (honest). No galactic position documented -> placeable=false, coords null.

Imperial Industrial World, the principal setting of 'Kingsblade' (Andy Clark): falls to a Word Bearers heresy and is retaken by the Adrastapol Knight Houses (the 'Betrayal on Donatos' / Donatos War). Existence solid (Lexicanum 'Betrayal on Donatos' + Fandom 'Donatos War'). Tags reflect Imperial ownership, the Adrastapol Imperial Knights who fight there, and the Word Bearers invaders. No Segmentum/sector documented -> sector null. No galactic position documented -> placeable=false, coords null.

Imperial ice world (a mining world in the Ironfound System, within the Damocles Gulf), the principal setting of 'Choose Your Enemies' (Sandy Mitchell), where Ciaphas Cain and the Valhallan 597th fight Eldar raiders and a Chaos cult. Existence confirmed via Lexicanum novel page. Sector 'ultima' on sector-level plausibility: the Damocles Gulf is documented as the Eastern-Fringe border region of Segmentum Ultima (consistent with the catalog's existing damocles_gulf -> ultima). Tags reflect Imperial ownership and the Astra Militarum (Valhallan 597th) presence. No galactic coordinates documented -> placeable=false, coords null.

Ocean/tithe-world used by the World Eaters (XII Legion) as a muster and recruitment fief-world during the Great Crusade/Horus Heresy; raided by Endryd Haar's loyalist Blackshields in The Red Fief — matches the source book exactly. No Segmentum/sector or galactic position documented, so sector and coords stay null (placeable:false).

Red, hyper-industrial throneworld of the Faash, taken to compliance by the joint Space Wolves / Dark Angels campaign that ended with the Lion executing the tyrant Durath (Dulan Campaign, 870.M30) — the setting of Leman Russ: The Great Wolf. No Segmentum/sector or documented galactic position, so sector and coords stay null. Historically a Faash throneworld but post-compliance status as a 'capital' is not evidenced for the map, so capital stays null.

Foetid/rotting world near the Red Scar and the Cicatrix Maledictum, within Imperium Nihilus, where the Red Wings (a Blood Angels successor) make planetfall and Astorath descends — the principal setting of Astorath: Angel of Mercy. Existence rests on the Lexicanum/Fandom novel coverage rather than a standalone world page, hence the slightly lower confidence. Proximity to the Great Rift does not pin one of the controlled Segmenta, so sector stays null.

Ork-infested cavern world cleansed by the Catachan II under Colonel 'Iron Hand' Straken in 926.M41 (the Dulma'lin Cleansing) — the setting of Straken. No Segmentum/sector or galactic position documented (the Ulani System is only the post-campaign redeployment, not Dulma'lin's location), so sector and coords stay null.

Agri-world from the Eisenhorn novel Hereticus, matching the source book (W40K-0003). Lexicanum places it in the Ophidian Subsector; Fandom confirms the Ophidian Subsector is a subsector of the Scarus Sector (Segmentum Obscurus), so sector=scarus. No documented galactic position -> placeable false, coords null.

Imperial world with the Mausolytic Precinct, subjugated by the Sons of Horus and defended by Iron Hands / Shattered Legions in the Horus Heresy short stories Little Horus (HH-0163) and The Either (HH-0277). Lexicanum lists its Segmentum as Unknown, so sector stays null. Heresy battle left it 'a smouldering ruin' but no clear planetary-destruction evidence -> destroyed null. No documented coords.

Tyranid-ravaged death world hosting Fabius Bile's laboratory in the Blood Angels novel Black Tide (W40K-0220). Lexicanum places it in the Ghoul Stars region, whose Segmentum is disputed/fuzzy (the catalog's own ghoul_stars entry is sector-null), so sector stays null rather than guessing ultima. Oceans/world survived the Tyranid predation -> destroyed null. No documented coords.

Imperial hive world of the Valedor system, last world standing against Hive Fleet Leviathan/Kraken and held by the Crimson Castellans in the novella The Last Days of Ector (W40K-0341). No Segmentum/sector is documented for the Valedor system in the cited sources, so sector stays null. No documented coords; final planetary fate not clearly stated -> destroyed null.

Imperial world Eisenhorn visits in Malleus (W40K-0002): a 'twist'/mutant world where, posing as buyers, he uncovers Inquisitorial collusion in the psyker trade. Lexicanum places Eechan in the Helican Subsector of the Scarus Sector, Segmentum Obscurus -- the Eisenhorn series' home region, the same cluster as the synopsis's companion worlds Gudrun and Sameter -> sector 'helican' (the precise, evidenced subsector; catalog already uses 'helican' for e.g. thracian_primaris). 'Daemon-touched' in the synopsis reflects the daemonhost Cherubael's presence, not a warp world -> warp null. No documented galactic position -> placeable false, coords null.

Paradise World (Great Crusade designation One-Forty-One-Nineteen) crowned by the Great Selenic palace-fortress at its northern pole. During the Horus Heresy the Emperor's Children occupied the Great Selenic; in Ironfire (HH-0199) Warsmith Idriss Krendl's Iron Warriors besiege it to test the Ironfire protocol and gather siege data for the assault on Terra (setting of Rob Sanders' 'Ironfire'). A dedicated Lexicanum page exists under exactly this spelling ('Euphoros'). Tagged with both legions present at the siege (EC occupiers + IW besiegers). No documented segmentum/sector -> sector null. The palace is brought down but the world is not stated destroyed -> destroyed null. No documented galactic position -> placeable false, coords null.

Imperial Knight World, home of House Callivant, settled before the Imperium and mined to exhaustion; longstanding ties to the Iron Hands and the Adeptus Mechanicus drive the tag set. Central setting of David Guymer's 'The Voice of Mars' (W40K-0461), where Iron Father Kristos recovers a Dawnbreak relic amid an Eldar-steered Ork invasion and inter-clan war. Lexicanum states Fabris Callivant lies in the Segmentum Obscurus -> sector 'obscurus' (an evidenced vocab sector). Lexicanum titles the article 'Fabris Callivant' (double-l) while the novel and this sentinel use 'Fabris Calivant' (single-l) -> same world, canon spelling variant; canonicalName kept as the novel/sentinel spelling, maintainer may prefer the Lexicanum form. No documented galactic position -> placeable false, coords null.

Imperial battlefield world littered with ancient battlefields; principal setting of Robbie MacNiven's 'Oaths of Damnation' (W40K-0582), where the Exorcists' Hexbreakers hunt the escaped Broken One/Red Marshal (the Khornate daemon Caedus, defeated there in an earlier age) while a Word Bearers warband under Dark Apostle Mordun contests it. Existence + canonical identity are carried by the Lexicanum 'Oaths of Damnation (Novel)' and 'Exorcists' articles, which both name the Fidem IV engagement -- a credible Lexicanum hit (the convention requires a credible hit, not a dedicated page) -> decision 'new'. No segmentum/sector is documented anywhere -> sector null (a sector-less but canonically-identified world, like many catalog battlefield worlds). Tagged Imperium + the Word Bearers raiders present. No documented galactic position -> placeable false, coords null.

Lexicanum '47-16' (Imperial compliance designation; the book spells it 'Forty-Seven Sixteen'): a human world that worshipped the Emperor as a god (via a copy of the Lectitio Divinitatus) and begged to join the Imperium, but the Word Bearers under Kor Phaeron / Lorgar declared the populace heathen idolaters and brought it to compliance with a 24-hour bombardment that razed every city save the shielded capital and massacred the planetary leaders — matching the 'Scions of the Storm' compliance / smoking-ruins setting. No Segmentum/sector or galactic position documented on Lexicanum, so sector/coords null and not placeable. Tagged imperium (Emperor-worshipping aspirant populace) + word_bearers (the compliance force). 'destroyed' left null rather than asserted: cities were devastated but the planet/capital persisted, so a hard destroyed=true is not cleanly evidenced the way it is for a cracked world like Caliban or Nostramo.

Lexicanum 'Furia Penitens': an Imperial Penal World (barren, with large jagged mountain ranges and deep valleys) holding prisoners from worlds such as Vostroya, Pan and Savlar. The 77th Vostroyan Firstborn were deployed there to crush a rebellion led by Colonel Strannik — exactly the prison-hive uprising and disgraced-Vostroyan setting of Andy Hoare's novel 'Commissar'. Dedicated Lexicanum page confirms existence. No Segmentum/sector or coordinates documented, so sector/coords null and not placeable. Tagged imperium + astra_militarum (Imperial penal world / deployed Vostroyan Guard regiment).

Principal world of Denny Flowers' recent novel 'Ghazghkull Thraka: Warlord of Warlords' — a Blood Axe world offered as tribute, where Ghazghkull stages his showdown with the Black Templars. The novel's Lexicanum page summarises the plot and names 'a Blood Axe world' as the climactic site, and GW's own Warhammer Community names it 'the Blood Axe world of Gabal' — so existence and the ork / Blood-Axe context are credibly established. No dedicated Lexicanum/Fandom entity page yet exists for the world itself (checked both), and no Segmentum/sector or galactic position is documented, so sector/coords stay null and not placeable. The catalog has no Blood Axes faction id, so tagged generically 'orks'. Confidence held modest pending a standalone world page; structural fields are deliberately null rather than guessed.

The book's 'Gathis' is the Doom Eagles Chapter homeworld, canonically 'Gathis II' (Lexicanum) — their fortress-monastery the Eyrie sits atop the Ghostmountain in the Razorpeaks. The Doom Eagles are a Loyalist Second Founding successor of the Ultramarines. Matches 'The Returned' (Veteran Sergeant Tarikus's unwelcome homecoming on the Doom Eagles' world). Not in the catalog under any id/alias, so decision=new with canonicalName 'Gathis II' (rawName 'Gathis' is the in-text short form). No Segmentum/sector or coordinates documented, so sector/coords null and not placeable (rather than guessing Ultima from the Ultramarines-successor lineage). Tagged imperium + doom_eagles; capital left null, consistent with other Chapter homeworlds in the catalog (Baal, Nocturne, Fenris).

Industrial world of the Gerat System, Agritha Subsector of the Chiros Sector, which lies in Segmentum Tempestus (Lexicanum) -> sector=tempestus. The whole Geratomro warzone of Shadowsword (Guy Haley) is its central setting. Imperial secessionist world reconquered in the Geratomran Reconquest; the Emperor's Children daemon-world plot was foiled by Black Templars + Shadowswords, so the world was not destroyed (destroyed=null). No documented galactic position -> placeable=false, coords null. System-capital status only; not a notable sector/realm capital -> capital=null.

World at the centre of the Ghaslakh xenohold, an Ork Empire at the end of the Great Crusade, in the Eastern Fringe rimward of Sotha and at the borders of Ultramar (Lexicanum). Anchor world Sotha and the Veridian/Calth muster are Segmentum Ultima -> sector=ultima (Eastern-Fringe edge of Ultima, sector-plausible). The greenskin hunt in the ruined Ghaslakh expanse is Alcaeus's on-page action in Hubris of Monarchia. In-universe sources doubt the xenohold ever existed, but it is a canonically named location; no documented galactic position -> placeable=false, coords null. Ork world -> tags=[orks].

Imperial mining world (enderrium ore, supplier to Forge World Shallethrax), defended by the Imperial Fists; the whole cult-purge of Fist of the Imperium unfolds here. Placed deep within Segmentum Solar (Lexicanum/Fandom), consistent with the synopsis's cult 'reaching towards Terra itself' -> sector=solar (moderate confidence; segmentum not a direct quote). Capital hive Angelicus is the planet's own seat, not a sector capital -> capital=null. Isolated by the Great Rift but not destroyed -> destroyed=null. No documented galactic position -> placeable=false, coords null.

Primary settled world of the Gildar System, which lies in Segmentum Obscurus, near the Gildar Rift (also Obscurus) -> sector=obscurus. The Gildar System is Silver Skulls territory; the surface struggle of The Gildar Rift (Sarah Cawkwell) against Huron Blackheart's Red Corsairs is decided here. Fought over but not destroyed -> destroyed=null. Gildar Secundus is a planet, not the warp rift itself -> warp=null. 'Primary' world status is not a notable sector capital -> capital=null. No documented galactic position -> placeable=false, coords null.

World in the Sabbat Worlds Cluster (Sabbat Region); during the Sabbat Worlds Crusade it was held by the Blood Pact (Chaos) and the subject of an Imperial reconquest led by the Volpone Bluebloods to liberate the Agria island chain. Matches the Volpone Glory source book. No documented galactic position -> sector-only, coords null.

Ork 'Scrapworld' (mobile/artificial planetoid built by Mekboyz) at the heart of the Telon Reach, the seat of a greenskin empire rivalling Ullanor. Assaulted during the Great Crusade with the Emperor and Horus fighting side by side (matches The Wolf of Ash and Fire); the Emperor decreed Gorro must be destroyed and its empire was put to flames -> destroyed: true. Telon Reach is not in the controlled sector vocab and no Segmentum is documented -> sector null (honest).

Imperial border world (Lexicanum: minor agri-world; the novel frames it as a mining world) in Ultima Segmentum, close to the Damocles Gulf, on the frontier with the T'au Empire and almost surrounded by T'au space. Setting of For the Emperor / the Gravalax Incident. Sector ultima is documented; only a regional position (near the Damocles Gulf) is given, no galactic coordinates -> placeable false, coords null.

A techno-barbarian state/region on Terra itself, in the northeastern part of the continent of Afrik (the territory of ancient Egypt), dating to the Age of Strife. The Sigillite recounts Khalid Hassan's covert raid into the wastelands of Gyptus (matches the source book). As a region of Terra it sits in Segmentum Solar; it is a sub-planetary location with no galactic coordinates -> placeable false, coords null.

Recruiting/ancestral world of the Blood Gorgons (Chaos Space Marine warband) in the Bastion Sector, principal setting of the novel Blood Gorgons (Henry Zou). Bastion Sector is not in the controlled vocab and no parent Segmentum is documented, so sector stays null. No documented galactic position — placeable false, coords null. Ravaged by a plague of mutation in the novel but not planet-destroyed (destroyed null).

Imperial Cardinal World, founded during the Great Crusade; setting of the Promethean War (980.M41) where the Salamanders and Order of the Ebon Chalice fought the Black Legion — the central campaign of Salamanders: Rebirth. Lexicanum lists it among the worlds of the Ultima Segmentum, so sector=ultima. No documented galactic coordinates (placeable false). War-torn but survived into M41, so destroyed null.

Sparsely populated desert world in the Segmentum Obscurus hosting a hidden Mechanicus weapons-research outpost; doomed world of Warrior Brood (C.S. Goto, Deathwatch), overrun by a Tyranid hive fleet. Lexicanum documents an Exterminatus ordered against the planet, so destroyed=true. No documented galactic coordinates (placeable false).

Ruined former shrine world, site of a battle between Rogal Dorn's Imperial Fists and the Iron Warriors during the Scouring; Dorn forbade it to rebuild, leaving it a 'ruinworld'. Principal setting of Helbrecht: Knight of the Throne, where the Black Templars liberate it from Iron Warriors. No Segmentum documented in the cited source, so sector stays null. Ruined but intact and liberated, not planet-destroyed (destroyed null). No documented galactic position (placeable false).

Hive Prome is named in Grotsnik: Da Mad Dok (Denny Flowers, 2025) as the last remaining Imperial holdout on a world ravaged by Beastboss Bakmun's Waaagh! — the book's named warzone. Only the novel blurb / Lexicanum novel page document it; no dedicated location page, no documented Segmentum/sector and no named parent world, so sector stays null and it is not placeable. Imperial hive city under ork assault (tags imperium + orks). Real, canonically-named setting but thinly sourced — lower confidence.

Lexicanum: Honoria is an Imperial Ice World located at the fringes of Segmentum Obscurus (sector obscurus). The Battle for Honoria pitted the Imperium against the Orks of Waaagh! Groblonik; the Dark Angels 5th Company was summoned under the Pact of Kulgotha — the war in which Ezekiel lost his eye (basis for the novel The Eye of Ezekiel). No documented galactic coordinates, so placeable false / coords null. Not in catalog; no alias match. Tags imperium + dark_angels + orks reflect the documented combatants.

Lexicanum: Iesta Veracrux is an Imperial world in the Taebian Sector, which is a sector of the Ultima Segmentum (sector ultima); its wine industry supplies the entire Ultima Segmentum, and it is governed by the Adeptus Arbites / the Sentine. In Nemesis (James Swallow) the Yosef Sabrat manhunt thread unfolds here. Imperial at the time of the novel (tags imperium); it only declared for the Warmaster later. No documented galactic position, so placeable false / coords null. Not in catalog; no alias match.

Lexicanum: Kadillus Harbour is the capital city of Piscina IV (capital: true at planet scale) and the focal settlement of the grinding Dark Angels-vs-ork siege in The Purging of Kadillus; Ghazghkull personally led the assault on its power plant. Piscina IV is a Segmentum Obscurus world (sector obscurus). It is a sub-planetary settlement distinct from the parent world Piscina IV (already in catalog as id piscina_iv), so a separate location is correct. No documented galactic coordinates, so placeable false / coords null. Tags imperium + dark_angels + orks reflect the documented combatants.

Kalidar is the star system of Guy Haley's 'Baneblade'; Lexicanum/Fandom place the Kalidar System in the Chiros Sector of Segmentum Pacificus (mapped to vocab 'pacificus'). Industrial world Kalidar IV was contested by the orks of Waaagh! Gratzdakka, hence imperium+orks tags. No documented galactic position, so placeable:false and coords null.

Kallash is the Imperial Feudal world (forested, the synopsis' 'forest world') of Steve Lyons' 'Iron Resolve', infested by feral orks from a crashed ship and defended by the Mordian Iron Guard in the Dirkr Forest. Lexicanum confirms existence; no Segmentum/sector is documented, so sector stays null (honest sector-unknown). No coordinates.

Kanai Tertius is the warzone of Robbie MacNiven's 'Vagabond Squadron' (2025); the Imperial Navy's 901st Tactical Wing fought an ork air invasion there (a Sky Gargant led the ork armada). Existence confirmed via Lexicanum's novel page and Sky Gargant page; no Segmentum/sector documented for this recent setting, so sector null and no coordinates.

Khadar is the parched, near-lifeless desert world of Lucien Soulban's 'Desert Raiders', where a Tallarn Desert Raiders regiment answers a distress call and is overrun by a Tyranid swarm (imperium+tyranids tags). Lexicanum's novel page confirms existence; no Segmentum/sector is documented for Khadar (the Tallarn regiments range Tempestus/Ultima, but that does not place Khadar), so sector null and no coordinates.

Lexicanum confirms Kiros as an invaded Imperial Administratum World defended by the Order of Our Martyred Lady alongside the Triumph of Saint Katherine against the forces of Chaos (Nurgle in the source novel) — matches the W40K-0114 / W40K-0578 context exactly (disambiguated from the similarly-named Krios/Kyros worlds). Categorised under Segmentum Unknown, so sector stays null; no galactic position documented (placeable false, coords null). World was held by the Imperium, not destroyed.

Lexicanum confirms Kolovan as an Imperial Industrial World assaulted by Tyranids of Hive Fleet Kraken; the Ultramarines' 3rd and 5th Companies under Chaplain Ortan Cassius defended it — matches the W40K-0399 (Cassius) context. Categorised under Segmentum Unknown, so sector stays null; no galactic position documented (placeable false, coords null). The Tyranids were ultimately repelled though the world was left poisoned and depopulated; not physically destroyed, so destroyed stays null rather than over-claiming a true.

Kronus is the Imperial Civilised World contested in the Dark Crusade; 'Inheritor' (HH-0207) is its canonical prequel, set 'deep within the great realm of Ultramar' during the Shadow Crusade with Word Bearers (Eliphas / Ark of Testimony) and World Eaters warbands — both tagged from the source synopsis. Ultramar lies in Segmentum Ultima, so sector = ultima (well-evidenced via the Ultramar placement). No documented galactic coordinates (placeable false). World survived and returned to Imperial rule after the Dark Crusade, so destroyed stays null.

The Kurbynola System is the canonical setting of the Deathwatch novel (Ian St. Martin) — Watch Captain Artemis' kill-team caught between an ork Waaagh! and a Tyranid hive fleet — matching the W40K-0426 context; tagged imperium/orks/tyranids accordingly. Existence is credible (Lexicanum novel page + Black Library + Fandom), but it has no dedicated lore page and is described only as being 'on the fringes of the galaxy', which does not pin it to a named segmentum from the vocab, so sector stays null and placeable false. Lower confidence reflects the thin structural data, not doubt about existence.

The Lastrati System (Nine Hollow Worlds) is the entire setting of 'Tyrant of the Hollow Worlds' — Imperial artificial planets attacked by Huron Blackheart's Red Corsairs, with Space Wolves intervening (hence contested imperium/chaos tags). Sector=obscurus is a sector-level inference: Lastrati is documented as a distant outpost under Forge World Lucius, which lies in Segmentum Obscurus; no direct Segmentum statement on the Lastrati page itself. No galactic coordinates documented (shrouded by the Siren Clouds miasma — not a warp feature) → placeable false.

Legitur is an Imperial Ecclesiarchy Scribe World devoted to producing holy scripture — the sole principal setting of 'Apostle' (Word Bearers Dark Apostle Cerastes vs. the Order of the Thorn under Palatine Aesura). Alignment Imperial is clear. Lexicanum lists its Segmentum as Unknown → sector stays null. No documented coordinates → placeable false.

Lentonia is the Imperial world where Ciaphas Cain and the Valhallan 597th are deployed (policing an insurrection, then a warp-tainted zombie outbreak) — the entire setting of the novella 'Old Soldiers Never Die'. Existence confirmed via the Lexicanum novella page; no dedicated world article, so no Segmentum/coordinates documented → sector null, placeable false. Alignment Imperial (Astra Militarum garrison + civilian population) is clear. The warp-tainted infection is a plot element, not a property of the world → warp null.

Lubentina is an Imperial shrine world (capital city Tharsis) housing the Warmason's Cathedral and the relics of Vadok Singh; it draws pilgrims and is defended by the Order of the Sombre Vow — the whole setting of 'Cult of the Warmason' where Battle-Sisters, an Iron Warriors warband, and the Cult of the Cataclysm genestealer cult collide. Alignment Imperial / Ecclesiarchy clear. No Segmentum or galactic coordinates documented → sector null, placeable false. 'capital' refers to a realm capital (cf. Terra/Macragge); Tharsis is Lubentina's planetary capital, not a galactic capital world → null.

Ocean/drowned shieldworld of the Cryptus System (Cryptan Shield), Ultima Segmentum, guarding the approach to Baal; Adepta Sororitas held the line vs Hive Fleet Leviathan. Consumed by the Tyranids during the Cryptus Campaign and rendered a Dead World -> destroyed: true. No documented galactic coordinates, so placeable: false, coords null. Matches the source book (Shield of Baal: Tempestus). Not in catalog (parent Cryptus system exists separately as 'cryptus').

Stellar region at the centre of the Machorta Sector, Segmentum Pacificus -> sector 'pacificus' (the named Machorta Sector is not in vocab). Site of the Battle of Machorta Sound early in the Indomitus Crusade, where a Khornate Slaughter Host dug in; matches Avenging Son's VanLeskus/Messinius theatre. A region, no documented coordinates -> placeable: false. Tagged 'region' per catalog convention. Not in catalog.

Daemon/fortress world held by the Iron Warriors under their Warsmith; the title world of the novel and the site of Lysander's imprisonment -> warp: true (a documented daemon world). Later fell to the Imperial Fists, who enacted Exterminatus (Fall of Malodrax) -> destroyed: true. No Segmentum/sector documented in the cited sources, so sector stays null (not guessed). placeable: false, coords null.

Homeworld of the Angels Resplendent Space Marine Chapter (Peter Fehervari's Dark Coil), capital city Kanvolis; setting of the novella The Reverie within The Dark Coil: Ascension -> matches the source book. Imperial Astartes homeworld -> tags imperium + angels_resplendent. No documented Segmentum/sector (Dark Coil locations are deliberately ambiguous) so sector null; warp anomaly is fiction-internal/ambiguous, left null. placeable: false, coords null. Not in catalog.

Death world in the Ultima Segmentum; the book's surface form 'Miral Prime' = canonical Lexicanum 'Miral' (Fandom: 'sometimes also known as Miral Prime'). Site of the Giant's Coffin mesa and a Scythes of the Emperor outpost where the shattered Chapter regrouped after Sotha fell (Hive Fleet Kraken). Sector ultima evidenced; no documented galactic coordinates, so placeable=false. Not physically destroyed (overrun, Scythes evacuated) -> destroyed null.

Imperial world (capital city Calaena) that survived the Age of Strife intact; discovered in the Great Crusade. In 'Magnus the Red: Master of Prospero' the Thousand Sons and Iron Warriors attempt an evacuation; Magnus and Perturabo enact Exterminatus to contain the Shai-Tan corruption -> destroyed=true. No segmentum/sector documented -> sector null (honest). Distinct from the 'Morningstar' audio drama; the planet matches the source book.

Imperial Adeptus Mechanicus forge world, principal setting of 'Day of Ascension' (Adrian Tchaikovsky); infested by the Genestealer Cult 'Congregation of the Divine Union'. No segmentum/sector documented -> sector null. Eventual Tyranid consumption is the cult's endgame but not flagged destroyed here (left to verifier-safe null). No documented coordinates -> placeable=false.

Contested world / necron Crownworld of the Khenisi Dynasty (an Imperial mining world in the 'Revenant Stars'); the embattled world Mephiston reaches in 'Mephiston: Revenant Crusade', locked in the Imperium-vs-Necron Revenant Crusade. 'Revenant Stars' is not one of the allowed vocab sectors -> sector null. Planet not destroyed (the necrons were burned out, the world persists) -> destroyed null. No documented coordinates -> placeable=false.

Desert World / former Terran colony brought to Imperial Compliance during the Great Crusade by the 670th Expeditionary Fleet; principal setting of 'Legion' (Abnett), the Alpha Legion counter-insurgency. Refused compliance and was destroyed by the Nurthene themselves via a Black Cube, leaving a lifeless Dead World -> destroyed: true. No documented Segmentum (Segmentum Unknown) -> sector null; no documented galactic position -> placeable false, coords null.

Imperial cemetery/burial world ravaged by a Chaos plague ~500 years prior; now a world of tombs, ossuaries and catacombs. Principal setting of 'The Relentless Dead' (Steve Lyons), where the 401st Death Korps of Krieg hunt a missing inquisitor in the bone-warrens. Still a functioning Imperial world (planetary governor, hallowed-rest vow) -> destroyed left null, not true. No documented Segmentum -> sector null; no documented galactic position -> placeable false.

Eldar-held world of Eldar-Human coexistence (inhabitants known as Ibsen), discovered by the Iron Hands 154th Expeditionary Fleet; brought to Compliance by Ferrus Manus, Vulkan and Mortarion in the Great Crusade. Principal setting of the 'Feat of Iron' novella (Kyme) where Ferrus is given the Eldar prophecy of his death. Lexicanum lists it under Segmentum Unknown -> sector null; no documented galactic position -> placeable false. Eventually scoured by flame and renamed Caldera (a Dead World); kept destroyed null since the book is set during the conquest, before the scouring. Not present in the catalog under One-Five-Four Four, Ibsen, or Caldera.

Imperial garrison world on the rim of Ultramar / Imperium Secundus, where Sergeant Aeonid Thiel is stranded on his way home to Macragge in the audio drama 'Red-Marked' (Kyme). Ultramar lies in the Ultima Segmentum, so sector set to 'ultima'; no documented galactic position -> placeable false, coords null. Tagged ultramarines as an Ultramar garrison world.

World of Ultramar in the Ultima Segmentum, an Ultramarines training planet. Heavily corrupted during the Plague Wars under Mortarion/Typhus/Ku'Gath and reclaimed by Guilliman — exactly the Plague War battlefield the synopsis describes. No documented galactic coordinates, so sector-only (ultima).

World of Ultramar in the Percepton System, Ultima Segmentum. Assaulted by Sor Talgron and Dark Apostle Jaruleth of the Word Bearers during the Shadow Crusade against the Ultramarines 17th Chapter under Aecus Decimus — the exact campaign of 'The Purge'. No documented coordinates; sector-only (ultima).

Cemetery world conquered by the Word Bearers, its surviving people forced to defile relics of the Imperial dead in mines — the setting of 'Masters of Shadow', where a Raven Guard Vanguard team under Turan Raakis wages a liberation war. Existence and context confirmed on Lexicanum; no Segmentum/sector or coordinates documented, so sector stays null.

Sparsely-inhabited world in the Ultima Segmentum of plateaux above lava-flooded lowlands; capital Principia Urbi on Principia Mons. Defended by Ciaphas Cain and the Valhallan 597th against a genestealer/Tyranid threat ('Duty Calls') — the book's principal setting. No documented coordinates; sector-only (ultima).

Distinct war-wracked planet, the principal setting of 'Lemartes: Guardian of the Lost', where Lemartes leads the Blood Angels Death Company against the Khornate Blood Disciples and banishes the Bloodthirster Skarbrand. NOT the Phlegethon River/Bridge on Armageddon (separate features). No documented segmentum/sector, so sector null; no documented galactic position, so placeable false / coords null. Tagged blood_angels (Death Company battleground) + chaos (Khornate Blood Disciples).

Dedicated Lexicanum page. Tranquil world at the far edge of the galaxy that threw off a Dark Eldar (xenos) occupation during the Age of Strife, then refused Imperial compliance; brought into compliance by the Night Lords under Konrad Curze (the setting of 'Konrad Curze: A Lesson in Darkness'). The source synopsis matches. No documented segmentum ('far edge of the galaxy' is not a controlled-vocab sector), so sector null; no documented galactic position, so placeable false / coords null. Tagged imperium (compliance world) + night_lords (conquering legion).

Dedicated Lexicanum page. Imperial Shrine World, the most prestigious Ecclesiarchy holding in the Tri-Sectors of Segmentum Obscurus (near the fringe of Segmentum Solar) -> sector 'obscurus'. Principal bulwark setting of 'Carcharodons: Outer Dark', defended against a tyranid splinter fleet and a Genestealer Cult uprising in its capital Pontifrax. No documented galactic position, so placeable false / coords null. Tagged imperium + ecclesiarchy (shrine world).

Dedicated Lexicanum page. Artificial World (believed built in the Dark Age of Technology) in the far east of the Ultima Segmentum near the borders of Ultramar -> sector 'ultima'. The laboratory setting of 'Genefather', where Archmagos Belisarius Cawl unveils his Pylon-network work and duels Fabius Bile. No documented galactic coordinates, so placeable false / coords null. Tagged imperium + mechanicus (Cawl's Adeptus Mechanicus showcase world).

Real, canonically-identified Imperial strategic shipyard world besieged by an Ork Attack Moon during the War of the Beast (The Beast Arises); the siege was lifted and the Moon destroyed, so the world survived (destroyed: false). Lexicanum documents the Sanctus System and its shipyards but does not clearly place the world in a Segmentum — only the relieving force is described as 'half of Segmentum Solar's naval strength', which is fleet origin, not a documented world location. Sector left null per 'a field you cannot evidence stays null'. No documented galactic position -> placeable false.

Real, canonically-identified Imperial capital world of the Gallows Cluster (capital city Evercity), garrisoned by the Cadian 101st after Cadia's fall and site of an early-M42 Heretic uprising (Cadian Honour). Lexicanum describes the Gallows Cluster as a backwater 'on the edges of the Segmentum Solar' -> sector solar. Capital of the Gallows Cluster -> capital true. World survived the uprising -> destroyed false. No documented galactic position -> placeable false.

Real, canonically-identified Imperial Hive World; during the Horus Heresy its governor Chravius Blumolotov pledged the world to the Warmaster and was killed by the Eversor Assassin Ganimus (Army of One). Canonically an Imperial Hive World -> tags [imperium]; the traitor turn is a transient plot event, not a standing alignment, so no chaos tag. No Segmentum documented on Lexicanum -> sector null. World not destroyed -> destroyed false. No documented galactic position -> placeable false.

Real, canonically-identified Imperial world besieged by the T'au, where the 597th deploys and Cain brokers an Imperium-T'au truce against Hive Fleet Kraken (The Greater Good). Documented on the marches of the Damocles Gulf, which lies in the Eastern Fringe of the Ultima Segmentum -> sector ultima. Contested between Imperium and T'au -> tags [imperium, tau]. World survived (unstable peace) -> destroyed false. No documented galactic position -> placeable false.

Desolate Imperial Fortress World, a bulwark against Ork warbands from the Jagal Stars; per Lexicanum located to the galactic south of Chogoris in Ultima Segmentum (sector=ultima). Site of the final battle of the Hunt for Voldorius (W40K-0280): its PDF was corrupted by the Alpha Legion and the world conquered by daemon prince Kernax Voldorius before the White Scars and Raven Guard cleansed it. No documented galactic coordinates -> placeable false, coords null. Distinct from catalog 'Valoria Quintus' and from 'Ghosar Quintus'.

Third planet of a five-world system around a Sol-like star; a former Garden/hive world depopulated by the Age of the Dark Imperium. Garrisoned by the Genesis Chapter (7th Company, frigate Excelsior) and attacked by the Iron Warriors of Bolaraphon/Dru'Kashyl during the Indomitus Crusade in Of Honour and Iron (W40K-0477). No Segmentum/sector documented in the cited source -> sector null (honest sector-unknown). No documented coordinates -> placeable false. 'destroyed' left null: a single virus-bomb warhead engulfed the surface, but the final planetary state is not crisply stated.

DEDUP hit. The shrine world Redemption (a.k.a. Redemption 219) of Cult of the Spiral Dawn / The Dark Coil: Damnation (W40K-0455 / W40K-0589) is the world Vytarn after its apocalypse; Vytarn's established Dark Coil nickname is 'the Candleworld'. The catalog already holds this world as id 'candleworld' (name 'Candleworld'), so the un-aliased surface form 'Redemption' aliases to it rather than creating a duplicate. Identity Redemption=Vytarn=Candleworld is well evidenced; the slightly reduced confidence reflects the inference that the catalog 'candleworld' entry is this same Vytarn.

Imperial fortress world of the Sanctus Line, a bulwark against incursions toward the inner Imperium; per Lexicanum in Segmentum Pacificus (Bastior Sub-sector) -> sector=pacificus. In Leviathan (W40K-0131) Lt Castamon of the Ultramarines defends it against a Hive Fleet Leviathan splinter fleet in the Fourth Tyrannic War; the climax destroys the world to deny it to the swarm ('Regium's destruction' / the 'Regium Breach') -> destroyed true. No documented coordinates -> placeable false, coords null.

Dedicated Lexicanum world page. Dying world, principal setting of the Death Korps of Krieg novel 'Death Rider' (Rhuairidh James); the 472nd Siege Regiment is dug in against the T'au Empire while Commissar Valian Hesh fights to make them retreat before Exterminatus. The planet's ecology collapses after a crashed flagship — but it is described as 'dying', not confirmed destroyed, so destroyed stays null. No Segmentum/sector documented for Rezlan VI itself (the Segmentum Tempestus reference belongs to Krieg's homeworld), so sector is null. No documented galactic position -> placeable false, coords null.

Real Imperial world, canonically the site of the 'Rhamiel Betrayal' (M41): Supreme Grand Master Naberius is ambushed and slain above Rhamiel and Azrael succeeds him — exactly the command crisis the 'Azrael' novel (Gav Thorpe) opens with. Dark Angels 8th and 10th Companies also put down uprisings on Rhamiel in 939.M41. Disambiguation: in the book context Rhamiel is the named world (not the Tactical Marine character of the same name). No Segmentum/sector or galactic position documented -> sector null, placeable false, coords null.
adjustments: existence refuted by verifier: Refute existence as a LOCATION: there is no canonical world named Rhamiel. The Lexicanum 'Rhamiel' disambiguation page lists exactly three entries — Rhamiel (Praetor, a character), Rhamiel (Tactical Space Marine, a character) and the Rhamiel Betrayal, which is explicitly 'a battle in M41' (an event), not a world. No Lexicanum/Fandom world page exists, and the betrayal's actual location is not canonically pinned to a planet named Rhamiel. The enricher's cited sources are the Rhamiel Betrayal event page and the Azrael character page — neither establishes a world. The only 'world of Rhamiel' wording comes from the Black Library novel blurb, which the convention bars as a basis for in-universe identity, and with two Dark Angels characters bearing the name the betrayal could be named after a person. Thin evidence for a location -> refute; the merge keeps it a sentinel.

locations.notes: Named Imperial world, the planet-wide civil-war setting of the novel 'Final Deployment' (R.S. Wilt, Black Library) where Tempestor Traxel's First Eradicant Tempestus Scions drop in; the traitor army is allied with the Iron Warriors. Evidence is novel-level only — there is no dedicated Lexicanum world page for Rilis, only the novel page naming it as the setting — hence the lower confidence. No Segmentum/sector or galactic position documented -> sector null, placeable false, coords null.

Dedicated Lexicanum world page. A quiet Imperium Agri World (mines and promethium refineries after organic-mineral deposits were found) that fell to Warboss 'Eadbasha's Ork Waaagh — the breakout setting of the novella 'Prisoners of Waaagh!' (Justin Woolley). Imperial world with a persistent Ork incursion -> tags imperium + orks (armageddon-style contested tagging). No Segmentum/sector or galactic position documented -> sector null, placeable false, coords null.

Shrine/memorial world held by the Blood Angels, awarded to them after the Phaedra Campaign; the climactic Arkio Insurrection of Deus Sanguinius is fought here. Lexicanum lists the Segmentum as Unknown, so sector stays null. Ruined-but-extant pilgrimage world (not Exterminatus-destroyed), so destroyed=null. No documented galactic position -> placeable false, coords null.

Sea world tied to the Carcharodons (setting of Silent Hunters); per Lexicanum's Tangata Manu / Void Glass articles the Chaplain hid the Void Glass and lived under the alias Tangaroa on Sagaraya. No dedicated Lexicanum page (mentioned within related articles), hence moderate confidence. No documented Segmentum -> sector null; no documented position -> placeable false, coords null.

Imperial ocean / industrial mining world, the setting of War of Secrets; invaded by the T'au in early M42 and contested by the Dark Angels. Lexicanum + the T'au-border context place it in the Ultima Segmentum (Eastern Fringe) -> sector ultima (sector-level plausibility). No documented galactic position -> placeable false, coords null.

Former Imperial Agri World in the Sandava System; the central battle of Warden of the Blade is fought here against a Slaaneshi daemonic incursion (~999.M41). The Grey Knights enacted Exterminatus upon it -> destroyed=true (evidenced). Segmentum not documented -> sector null. No documented galactic position -> placeable false, coords null.

Real, canonically-identified world: the Grey Knights under Castellan Garran Crowe fight on Sandava III (after Sandava II) against the forces of Slaanesh c.999.M41, and the planet is swallowed by the Cicatrix Maledictum/Great Rift warp storm — the setting of the novel Castellan. No segmentum/sector or galactic position is documented, so sector and coords stay null (placeable false). Tagged imperium + grey_knights for the contested Imperial world; 'destroyed' left null (the world was displaced/cut off by the warp storm, not confirmed destroyed).

Real, canonically-identified region of Imperial space (a 'Reach', not a single world): the War for the Sargassion Reach (991.M41) saw a Chaos warband invade and the Raven Guard 4th Company with the Brazen Minotaurs counter-attack — the frame of the anthology Sons of Corax. No segmentum is documented (described only as near the Kandoor System), so sector stays null and it is not placeable. Tagged region + imperium + raven_guard.

Real, canonically-identified world from the Great Crusade: the Dark Angels are sent to bring Sarosh to compliance (relieving the White Scars); the Saroshi secretly worshipped Chaos entities (the 'Melachim'), and the Legion forcibly took the world after killing the manifesting daemon — the final-act setting of Descent of Angels (Horus Heresy #6). No segmentum/sector or position is documented, so sector and coords stay null (placeable false). Tagged imperium + dark_angels (compliance world taken by the Lion's Legion); 'destroyed' left null.

Real, canonically-identified world: a wealthy Imperial planet cut off from the Imperium by warp storms, invaded and occupied for eight years by Xantine and his Emperor's Children warband the Adored (who tried to reshape it after Chemos), before being overrun by Tyranids — the central setting of Renegades: Lord of Excess. No segmentum/sector or galactic position is documented, so sector and coords stay null (placeable false). Tagged chaos + emperors_children for the warband's occupation that frames the book. 'warp' left null — it is a material world isolated BY warp storms, not itself a warp feature.

Canonical world in 'Ephrael Stern: The Heretic Saint' (Daemonifuge arc), set in the Era Indomitus on the far side of the Great Rift (Imperium Nihilus) — no vocab sector maps to that, so sector stays null. Contested between the Order of Our Martyred Lady and a Word Bearers warband, with a Necron 'silent/frozen doom' threat. No documented galactic coordinates -> placeable false.

Hive World in the Contqual Subsector, the central battleground of 'Wrath of Iron' (Purging of Contqual). Lexicanum lists the Contqual Subsector as Segmentum Unknown, so no vocab sector is evidenced -> sector null. Imperial hive world that fell to Slaanesh worship and was purged by the Iron Hands; a warp rift opened on-world. No documented coordinates -> placeable false.

Ice World, the sole setting of the Ciaphas Cain novel 'Caves of Ice' — Valhallan 597th defend a promethium refinery against an Ork warband while a Necron tomb stirs beneath the permafrost. Lexicanum gives no segmentum (Segmentum Unknown), so sector null. No documented coordinates -> placeable false.

Swamp world, principal setting of 'Flesh and Iron' (Bastion Wars #2). Ecclesiarchy-controlled Imperial world whose indigenous Carnibales rose up, armed and led by the Blood Gorgons' Dos Pares (Chaos) -> imperium+chaos tags. The Bastion Wars campaign / Medina Corridor sits in the Eastern galactic regions = Ultima Segmentum, so sector=ultima at sector-level plausibility (no on-world coordinates documented -> placeable false). Sector evidence is the campaign's segmentum, hence the moderate confidence.

Lexicanum 'Styxia Prime' = an Imperial Agri World in the Eastern Fringes defended against Hive Fleet Gorgon by Cadian forces and the Ultramarines' Master of Sanctity Ortan Cassius — exactly the Catechism of Hate scenario (Cassius holding the world vs the Tyranid swarm). The book shortens it to 'Styxia'; canonical display name is 'Styxia Prime'. Sector left null: Lexicanum places it in the 'Eastern Fringes', which is not one of the vocab segmenta/sectors, so not safely mappable. Defended successfully → destroyed:false. No documented galactic position → placeable:false, coords null.

Lexicanum 'Targian' = a remote Imperium Hive World where Zelia Lor's mother runs an archaeotech dig; the Necron Ketatrix Dynasty invaded after the Diadem of Transference was unearthed and destroyed the world — the exact setting/inciting event of Attack of the Necron (Warhammer Adventures). destroyed:true is well evidenced. Sector left null: one search reported 'Segmentum Pacificus' but two others gave no segmentum and the Lexicanum page is paywalled to direct fetch, so the segmentum is not reliably evidenced. No documented position → placeable:false, coords null.

Tatricala is the named world on which Lord Commander Eidolon (Emperor's Children) is marooned in the Horus Heresy novel Eidolon: The Auric Hammer — confirmed by the Lexicanum novel article. It is a canonically named world (real Black Library novel) so existence holds, but no dedicated location article exists and no segmentum/position is documented → sector null, placeable:false, coords null. Tags reflect the Chaos / Emperor's Children presence in the story. Lower confidence: existence rests on the novel article rather than a standalone location entry.

Telken's Rest is an ice-locked world at the heart of the Chaos warlord Drakul-zar's regime; the Cadian 101st (Minka Lesk) deployed there to seize Warp Routes and secured its central city after the Scourged warband retreated, capturing Drakul-zar — exactly the Shadow of the Eighth setting. Mentioned in the canonical Lexicanum articles for Drakul-zar and The Scourged (no dedicated location page). Tags: Scourged = chaos; Cadian 101st = astra_militarum/cadia. No segmentum/position documented → sector null, placeable:false, coords null. Captured rather than destroyed → destroyed:null (not evidenced as destroyed).

Lexicanum confirms Tempest as an ancient Imperial Shrine World, the objective of the Black Templars' Second Dorean Crusade in 'Broken Crusade' (W40K-0206) — matches the source book's context. Described as lying on the fringes of the Cicatrix Maledictum, which crosses the galaxy and does not pin a Segmentum, so sector stays null. No documented galactic position -> placeable false, coords null. It was overrun by Khorne warbands but ultimately liberated, not destroyed (destroyed left null rather than guessed).

Thawra is the canonically-named human world Malcador investigates in the BL Horus Heresy short story 'Prologue to Nikaea' (HH-0223) by David Annandale, where a psyker civilisation has organised itself into warring witch-armies — matches the source book. Lexicanum documents it via the Prologue to Nikaea page. A Great Crusade-era human world (imperium tag), but no Segmentum/sector is documented -> sector null; no documented position -> placeable false, coords null. 'Burning provinces' implies devastation but not planetary destruction, so destroyed stays null.

Thoas has a dedicated Lexicanum page (plus 'Battle of Thoas' / 'Ork Emperor of Thoas'): a dead world, a human civilisation lost in Old Night, later the seat of an ork empire that Roboute Guilliman's XIII Legion purged in the Great Crusade — exactly the campaign in 'Roboute Guilliman: Lord of Ultramar' (HH-0081). Tags reflect the combatants (Ultramarines vs orks), matching catalog convention for contested worlds. No Segmentum documented in the sources -> sector null; no documented position -> placeable false, coords null. Only the planet's ruins were flattened by orbital bombardment (not the planet itself), so destroyed stays null.

Lexicanum (Hell Night short-story page) confirms Vaporis as an Imperial monsoon world whose capital is Aphium, where the Salamanders (Dak'ir, Tsu'gan, Pyriel) reinforce the Imperial Guard 135th Phalanx against a Chaos-tainted secessionist rebellion — matches 'Hell Night' (W40K-0409). Tags reflect the Imperial world and the Salamanders campaign. No Segmentum/sector documented in the sources -> sector null; no documented galactic position -> placeable false, coords null. 'capital' (Aphium) is a city on Vaporis, not Vaporis being an Imperial capital world, so the capital boolean stays null.

Canonical Lexicanum entry: the fields of Velbayne were the site of a Horus Heresy battle between the Space Wolves Legion (Leman Russ and the Rout) and an army of daemons — the setting of 'Bjorn: Lone Wolf'. No documented Segmentum/sector or galactic position, so sector=null and placeable=false (honest sector-less location). Imperium / Space Wolves tags from the defending force.

Canonical Lexicanum entry: shrine world of the Golden Chain, the last to remain defiant during the Indomitus Crusade; principal setting of 'The Martyr's Tomb', defended by the Order of Our Martyred Lady and the Black Templars against Nurgle/Death Guard cultists. The Golden Chain is documented (Lexicanum) as lying in the Cirillo Sector of Segmentum Ultima → sector=ultima. Imperial shrine world held by the Sororitas/Black Templars → imperium + ecclesiarchy + adepta_sororitas tags. No documented galactic position → placeable=false. Holds the line (not destroyed) → destroyed=null.

Capital of the Vincula Sub-sector, garrisoned by the Tanith First-and-Only and the principal setting of Dan Abnett's 'The Vincula Insurgency' (Gaunt's Ghosts prequel). The Vincula Sub-sector sits within the Sabbat Worlds (Segmentum Pacificus rimward) → sector=sabbat_region, matching the catalog's other Sabbat-region worlds. Explicitly the sub-sector capital → capital=true. No dedicated Lexicanum/Fandom article and no documented galactic position → placeable=false. Imperium / Astra Militarum tags.

Capital world of the T'au Farsight Enclaves ('Children of Vior'la'), Farsight's main base; the home world devoured by tyranids in 'Farsight: Blade of Truth'. The Farsight Enclaves lie in Segmentum Ultima, on the far side of the Damocles Gulf in the Eastern Fringe → sector=ultima (consistent with the catalog's tau_empire/octarius Ultima placements). Xenos / T'au tags, capital=true. Under tyranid assault but not canonically confirmed destroyed → destroyed=null; no documented coordinates → placeable=false.

Lexicanum: Agri-World in an isolated area of the Dominion of Storms in the Ultima Segmentum; setting of James Swallow's HH short story 'Liar's Due', where an Alpha Legion operative breaks the world with rumour alone. Sector = ultima (Segmentum-level, documented). No documented galactic position -> placeable false, coords null. World tears itself apart in civil strife but is not canonically destroyed -> destroyed null.

Lexicanum 'Viridia (Agri World)': a productive Agri-World in the Ultima Segmentum, hub of mining stations on surrounding moons/asteroids; site of the Viridian Insurrection (genestealer cult / Spawn of Damnation hulk) cleansed by the Reclaimers Chapter -- matching the Cain novel 'The Emperor's Finest'. Sector = ultima (documented). No documented galactic position -> placeable false, coords null. World survives (cleansed) -> destroyed null.

Lexicanum 'Visage' (also known as Pythonissa): a psychically active swamp/death world of briny seas and endless fogs, its primary value to the Imperium being its tithe of psykers; principal setting of Steven B. Fischer's 'Witchbringer' (Glavia Aerand / Cadian 900th). No Segmentum/sector documented on the page -> sector null (honest). No documented galactic position -> placeable false, coords null.

Lexicanum (House Cadmus / Knights of the Imperium): Vondrak is a Forge World defended by House Cadmus against the Tyranids of Hive Fleet Hydra in 999.M41 (following the fall of Gryphonne IV), with Archmagos/Adept Nemonix of Mars contesting control. Setting of Graham McNeill's novella 'Knights of the Imperium'. Forge World -> tags imperium + mechanicus. No Segmentum/sector documented for Vondrak itself -> sector null. No documented galactic position -> placeable false, coords null.

Documented as the distant secessionist colony world the Brimlock Eleventh are promised in Imperial Glory, overrun by a feral ork infestation from a crashed rok — matches the source book exactly. No Segmentum/sector is documented in any source, so sector stays null (honest sector-unknown world). placeable false, no coordinates. tags reflect the Imperium-vs-orks colonial war on the world.

The Custodes-investigated world of Auric Gods, documented as a world 'at the fringe of the Sol system' — placing it in Segmentum Solar (sector=solar) with moderate confidence; the description is regional rather than a precise sector citation. Imperial world (peacekeepers, Custodes investigation) so tag imperium. The world is in self-annihilation in the story but no source confirms full destruction, so destroyed stays null. No documented galactic position → placeable false, coords null.

Vytarn, the storm-wracked sanctuary world of the Last Candle (Order of the Eternal Candle) in Requiem Infernal / The Dark Coil: Ascension, is canonically and repeatedly 'referred to as the Candleworld' (later Redemption-219). The catalog already contains this world under id 'candleworld' (name 'Candleworld'), so this is an alias of an existing entity, not a new one — aliasTo candleworld to avoid a duplicate.

Documented prison-forge / experimental penitentiary station orbiting the Forge World Xana II (transplanted from its earlier position Xana V), the raided setting of Blackshields: The False War where Endryd Haar's Blackshields strike during the Xana Incursion. Xana II sits in the Vicidax Thule Sector on the western edge of Segmentum Pacificus → sector=pacificus. Mechanicum forge installation (it 'chose the Warmaster') → tag mechanicus. No documented galactic coordinates → placeable false, coords null. The station fell under assault but is not clearly recorded as destroyed (Xana II later survives as a Hell-Forge), so destroyed stays null.

Colonial capital city of the world Zalidar, the besieged settlement Calgar fights across country to reach and hold in Calgar's Siege (Paul Kearney). Named as 'the city of Zalathras' on the Lexicanum novel page. As a settlement on Zalidar it inherits the Segmentum Ultima placement of Ultramar (Zalidar is an outlying colony of Ultramar; Ultramar lies in Segmentum Ultima). capital=true: explicitly the colonial capital. No documented galactic position -> placeable false, coords null. Not in the catalog or alias map.

Remote outlying colony world of Ultramar, sole setting of Calgar's Siege (Paul Kearney); Marneus Calgar's goodwill tour coincides with an Ork invasion. Lexicanum novel page describes it as a colony of Ultramar; Ultramar lies in Segmentum Ultima (id 'ultima'), so sector is well-evidenced by deduction rather than guessed. Its capital is the separate city Zalathras, so Zalidar itself is not flagged capital. No documented galactic coordinates -> placeable false, coords null. Not present in locations.json or location-aliases.json.

Dedicated Lexicanum page exists. World lying fairly close to Caliban in the Segmentum Obscurus (id 'obscurus'), site of the Great Crusade-era Zaramund Campaign (Dark Angels under Luther, Luna Wolves, Death Guard) and, in Exocytosis (HH-0213), Luther's encampment where his Dark Angels grant the fleeing Death Guard safe harbour. Synopsis and search both place it in Segmentum Obscurus. Tags reflect the two factions present in the source book. No documented galactic coordinates -> placeable false, coords null. Not in the catalog or alias map.

Dedicated Lexicanum page exists. Former Civilised/Mining World re-designated a Penal World of the Imperium, tithing adamantium; central setting of Carcharodons: Red Tithe (W40K-0442). No Segmentum or named sector is documented on Lexicanum or Fandom, so sector stays null per convention (honest, not guessed). Imperial-affiliated (Adeptus Arbites garrison, Imperial Commander) -> tag 'imperium'; the Night Lords and Carcharodons only raid it. No documented galactic position -> placeable false, coords null. Not in locations.json or location-aliases.json.

Eldar craftworld that fled into the void to escape the Fall and returned from sanctuary in the warp; principal setting of Ghost Warrior (Rise of the Ynnari), the book that anchors this sentinel. Lexicanum titles the article 'Zaisuthra'; the novel and Black Library descriptions use 'Ziasuthra' (the source surface form), so canonicalName follows the book. As a mobile craftworld it has no documented Segmentum/sector and no canonical galactic position -> sector/coords null, placeable false. Not destroyed (it returned).

Compliance world brought into the Imperium during the Great Crusade by the Night Lords and Thousand Sons ('Devastation of Zoah'); setting of the recounted Curze/Magnus clash in The Abyssal Edge. Imperium-aligned. Lexicanum lists its Segmentum as Unknown -> no documented sector and no canonical position, so sector/coords null, placeable false. The Tower of Serenity was the contested bombardment target; the world itself is not documented as destroyed -> destroyed null.
